comments.author: ddl-grequeni
comments.body: This is kind of a critical problem with this method. If new branches have been created in the remote but none of the existing ones have been modified, then this method doesn't update the clone, and that means the new branches take up to 10 minutes to show up in repoman responses

pr_title: DOM-54603 Add logs to repoman

comments.author: ddl-grequeni
comments.body: Weird behavior, if it failed last time, we don't queue it again. But next time we do queue it (because we delete the failure cached value). So 2 consecutive almost concurrent calls to this end up updating the clone anyways. Why bother having this failure cache?

comments.author: ddl-mhito
comments.body: Maybe due to the retry policy of messages through Domino Messaging? The retry for this particular method is twice. Maybe this handles the edge case where the clone fails, but the message responses fails to send or receive, so the message is sent again, and we don't want to try the clone failing again.

comments.author: ddl-grequeni
comments.body: not sure I follow. which messages? who would be the sender/receiver?

comments.author: ddl-mhito
comments.body:
The only time it's possible for a branch to not exist on the remote is if it has been deleted on the remote in the time when the clone was present in the clone cache. I think that the fileMetaDataCache (which the branches are taken from) is guaranteed to contain only the cloned branches. 

I don't believe that repoman ever directly modifies this local clone without modifying the upstream (like creating a new branch).

comments.author: ddl-grequeni
comments.body: I was thinking of what happens if the branch is deleted in the remote, not through repoman I mean.

pr_title: [DOM-54272] Add central config value for Flows

comments.author: noahjax
comments.body:
We really shouldn't have any dependencies on `server` in `jobs-implementation`. I know that this dependency was added [here](https://github.com/cerebrotech/domino/pull/31741/files) and not in your PR, but I am still reluctant to expand the extend to which this depends on `server`.

The whole idea of having `jobs-implementation` and `jobs-monolith-adapter` is that we can isolate all dependencies on `server` to `jobs-monolith-adapter` and keep `jobs-implementation` code clean. Then over time we can purge more and more dependencies on `server` until we can get rid of the adapter code altogether. While this will never happen because we don't prioritize any work to purge deps, I don't want to move backwards.

Some other options:
- Utilize the existing class we have for checking settings outside of `jobs-monolith-adapter`, `SettingsAdapter`. This class is used in a number of places already, including `JobsController`. You'd have to add another function to this class to check if flows is enabled. Personally I'm not a huge fan of any sort of logic in the controller, but in this case it's not terrible because it is really simple
  - Alternatively you could start injecting this `SettingsAdapter` into `InProcessJobsService` instead and keep the rest of your PR almost the same
- push this check into `JobLauncherAdapter`. It is a bit more annoying to do there because all jobs use the same `launchJob` function, but you can accomplish pretty much the same thing by checking for the existence of `workflowConfig`
- Inject this value as a named boolean. This would entail
  - defining a `def providesFlowsEnabled(settings: Settings): Boolean` in `JobsMonoAdapterInjectionModule`. You will need to use both the `@Provides` and `@Named` annotations here
  - consuming this named boolean here instead of all of settings via `@Named flowsEnabled: Boolean` in the class constructor
  - Refactoring tests, which will actually be cleaner now because you don't have to mock settings

pr_title: DOM-54603 Fix Repoman not seeing new branches in remotes + basic repoman unit tests

comments.author: ddl-grequeni
comments.body: This is fixing the original issue, return all branches found in the git fetch rather than filtering by the branches repoman knows about.

comments.author: niole
comments.body: Do you know why it used to only look at branches currently known by repoman? It doesn't make sense to me

comments.author: ddl-grequeni
comments.body: No, this is why I was so reluctant to change this logic and was thinking of adding a separate method, but then I realized the `staleBranches` are only used in one place, so editing this would only affect that place which is what we intended to change. Maybe this was used differently in the past and then usages changed/evolved but this method didn't. Also cucu tests around this used to pass because they were all using the same branches that repoman knew about, but when we started having a unique branch per test, then this issue came to light.

comments.author: niole
comments.body: You probably will need to add a default value for this in this application conf in charts: https://github.com/cerebrotech/charts/blob/master/domino/repoman/files/application.conf. I would check to see what happens in an fcmd deployment if you don't provide a default value. If you get some failures around the config value not being there, definitely add to the charts file. It's probably a good idea to add to the charts file anyway, so that all configuration values are transparent to engineers who work with charts.

comments.author: ddl-grequeni
comments.body:
Yes, thanks for pointing this out! It makes it fail with the EXPIRED_DEAD_LETTER error which would have been hard to debug if you hadn't commented this.
I'll add it to charts, but I think the APP should have a default anyways, I don't know how is that config file propagated to on prem customers, but in any case, if I add a new config that makes the APP break if not defined, is like adding a backwards incompatibility. So I added some fallback logic in case this is not present in the config file, it will default to true.
